message:
feat(scaffold): implement core module sources, tests, and platform stubs (bd-ix8.1)

Scaffold the asx ANSI C repository with compilable source code:

- Core implementations: status.c, transition_tables.c, budget.c,
  outcome.c, cancel.c with full transition authority tables, budget
  algebra, outcome join lattice, and cancel severity/budget lookups
- Test harness: minimal test_harness.h with ASSERT_EQ/TRUE/FALSE/STR_EQ
- Unit tests: test_status (4), test_transition (11), test_budget (8),
  status_basic_test (6) — all 29 tests pass with -Werror
- Platform stubs: posix/win32/freestanding hooks.c
- Runtime stubs: lifecycle.c, scheduler.c, quiescence.c, cancellation.c
- Channel/time stubs: mpsc.c, timer_wheel.c
- Reconcile type ownership: core headers now delegate enum/typedef
  definitions to asx_ids.h, eliminating duplicate declarations

All sources compile clean under gcc -std=c99 -Wall -Wextra -pedantic -Werror.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -115,7 +115,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-ix8","title":"EPIC: Build System, Portability Matrix, and Repository Scaffold","description":"Background:
The runtime must compile and behave predictably across host and constrained targets. Build portability is a first-class requirement, not cleanup work.

Goal:
Establish build/repo infrastructure for GCC/Clang/MSVC plus embedded cross-target matrix, including QEMU validation harness.

Must preserve:
- dependency-free core assumptions
- profile-aware compile paths
- reproducible, diagnosable failures

Why this serves users:
- HFT teams need deterministic reproducible builds
- embedded teams need cross-target confidence before deployment
- maintainers need fast feedback loops with clear failure surfaces.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:41.970107711Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:18:09.564256335Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","phase-2","portability"]}
 {"id":"bd-ix8.1","title":"Scaffold repository layout and module boundaries for include/src/tests/fixtures/tools/docs","description":"Background:
The codebase structure must enforce architectural boundaries from day one to prevent accidental coupling.

Subtasks:
- Create canonical directory map for include, src, tests, fixtures, tools, docs.
- Define ownership for each module boundary and allowed dependencies.
- Define naming conventions for APIs, internal files, and test fixtures.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Layout aligns with proposed architecture and parity tracking needs.
- No ambiguous module ownership.
- New contributors can infer where code belongs without guessing.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.042079992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:35:54.621282796Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T04:35:54.621264252Z","close_reason":"Completed scaffold contract details: canonical directory map, explicit module ownership/dependency boundaries, and naming conventions in docs/PHASE2_SCAFFOLD_MANIFEST.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","phase-2","structure"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-296.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.441421319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-296.30","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:46.237220433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.042079992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ix8.2","title":"Implement primary Makefile workflow and optional CMake generator strategy","description":"Background:
Build and test commands must be stable and scriptable across environments.

Subtasks:
- Define make targets for build, lint, format-check, test, conformance, fuzz-smoke, profile-parity.
- Document optional CMake generation path for consumers needing IDE/toolchain integration.
- Define reproducibility expectations (flags, target profiles, deterministic options).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Make targets cover all quality gates in plan section 10.6.
- Commands are CI-compatible and locally reproducible.
- Optional CMake path does not become the primary maintenance burden.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.120183292Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:42:13.473721408Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T04:42:13.473702843Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","cmake","make","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.120183292Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.518964269Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-ix8.3","title":"Implement compiler and platform matrix scripts (GCC/Clang/MSVC, 32/64-bit, endian variants)","description":"Background:
Portability failures found late are expensive and destabilizing. Matrix checks must be early and continuous.

Subtasks:
- Define matrix build scripts for GCC, Clang, and MSVC.
- Include 32-bit and 64-bit targets where supported.
- Add endian-variance checks and compile-time assumptions validation.
- Document expected warning policy and failure handling.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Matrix scripts are deterministic and produce parseable logs.
- Unsupported targets fail with actionable diagnostics.
- No compiler-specific assumptions leak into core without explicit guards.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.201876549Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:36.582981945Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","ci","phase-2","portability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.201876549Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.597742193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-ix8.3","title":"Implement compiler and platform matrix scripts (GCC/Clang/MSVC, 32/64-bit, endian variants)","description":"Background:
Portability failures found late are expensive and destabilizing. Matrix checks must be early and continuous.

Subtasks:
- Define matrix build scripts for GCC, Clang, and MSVC.
- Include 32-bit and 64-bit targets where supported.
- Add endian-variance checks and compile-time assumptions validation.
- Document expected warning policy and failure handling.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Matrix scripts are deterministic and produce parseable logs.
- Unsupported targets fail with actionable diagnostics.
- No compiler-specific assumptions leak into core without explicit guards.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.201876549Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:44:41.893300344Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","ci","phase-2","portability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.201876549Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.597742193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ix8.4","title":"Implement embedded cross-target matrix for router-class triplets","description":"Background:
Embedded usefulness must be validated on real target architectures, not inferred from host builds.

Subtasks:
- Add cross-build scripts for mipsel-openwrt-linux-musl, armv7-openwrt-linux-muslgnueabi, and aarch64-openwrt-linux-musl.
- Define profile flags and resource-class defaults for these targets.
- Capture binary-size and startup metrics needed for regression tracking.

Acceptance Criteria:
- All triplets build cleanly with reproducible logs.
- Failing targets report missing assumptions explicitly.
- Output artifacts are suitable for CI and smoke deployment.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.281753373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:36.448773735Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","embedded","openwrt","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.281753373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:31.547540399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.679854886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ix8.5","title":"Create QEMU harness for router-class scenario and replay validation","description":"Background:
Cross-compilation alone is insufficient; runtime behavior on target-like environments is required.

Subtasks:
- Build QEMU execution harness for key router-class targets.
- Run scenario DSL suites and replay checks under emulation.
- Emit detailed logs and artifact bundles for failed runs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- QEMU runs are deterministic and scriptable in CI.
- Harness surfaces semantic/parity regressions clearly.
- Artifact package includes stdout/stderr, trace digests, and config snapshot.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.362993962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:40.272217468Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["embedded","phase-2","qemu","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.362993962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:48:42.758483871Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ix8.6","title":"Implement compile-time memory-layout and budget-delta reporting per target","description":"Background:
Plan calls for per-target visibility into memory layout and budget drift to protect constrained deployments.

Subtasks:
- Generate compile-time reports for key structs and allocator budgets per target.
- Diff reports across target matrix and flag regressions.
- Integrate report artifacts into CI outputs.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Reports are reproducible and machine-comparable.
- Budget deltas are surfaced before deployment regressions.
- Embedded classes can be tuned with concrete evidence.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:19.388405193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:38.760608275Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","embedded","idea-20","memory"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:19.388405193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:52:20.567986842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ix8.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ix8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:31.151618622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 # Test sources
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 UNIT_TEST_SRC := $(wildcard tests/unit/core/*_test.c) \
+                 $(wildcard tests/unit/core/test_*.c) \
                  $(wildcard tests/unit/runtime/*_test.c) \
+                 $(wildcard tests/unit/runtime/test_*.c) \
                  $(wildcard tests/unit/channel/*_test.c) \
-                 $(wildcard tests/unit/time/*_test.c)
+                 $(wildcard tests/unit/channel/test_*.c) \
+                 $(wildcard tests/unit/time/*_test.c) \
+                 $(wildcard tests/unit/time/test_*.c)
+UNIT_TEST_SRC := $(sort $(UNIT_TEST_SRC))
 
 INVARIANT_TEST_SRC := $(wildcard tests/invariant/lifecycle/*_test.c) \
-                      $(wildcard tests/invariant/quiescence/*_test.c)
+                      $(wildcard tests/invariant/lifecycle/test_*.c) \
+                      $(wildcard tests/invariant/quiescence/*_test.c) \
+                      $(wildcard tests/invariant/quiescence/test_*.c)
+INVARIANT_TEST_SRC := $(sort $(INVARIANT_TEST_SRC))
 
 UNIT_TEST_BIN := $(patsubst tests/%.c,$(TEST_DIR)/%,$(UNIT_TEST_SRC))
 INV_TEST_BIN  := $(patsubst tests/%.c,$(TEST_DIR)/%,$(INVARIANT_TEST_SRC))

@@ -19,6 +19,7 @@
 #define ASX_IDS_H
 
 #include <stdint.h>
+#include <asx/asx_export.h>
 
 /* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
 /* Opaque handle types                                                */
@@ -89,4 +90,90 @@ static inline int asx_handle_state_allowed(uint64_t h, uint16_t allowed_mask)
     return (asx_handle_state_mask(h) & allowed_mask) != 0;
 }
 
+/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
+/* Time representation                                                */
+/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
+
+typedef uint64_t asx_time;
+
+/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
+/* Region lifecycle states                                            */
+/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
+
+typedef enum {
+    ASX_REGION_OPEN       = 0,
+    ASX_REGION_CLOSING    = 1,
+    ASX_REGION_DRAINING   = 2,
+    ASX_REGION_FINALIZING = 3,
+    ASX_REGION_CLOSED     = 4
+} asx_region_state;
+
+/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
+/* Task lifecycle states                                              */
+/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
+
+typedef enum {
+    ASX_TASK_CREATED          = 0,
+    ASX_TASK_RUNNING          = 1,
+    ASX_TASK_CANCEL_REQUESTED = 2,
+    ASX_TASK_CANCELLING       = 3,
+    ASX_TASK_FINALIZING       = 4,
+    ASX_TASK_COMPLETED        = 5
+} asx_task_state;
+
+/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
+/* Obligation lifecycle states                                        */
+/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
+
+typedef enum {
+    ASX_OBLIGATION_RESERVED  = 0,
+    ASX_OBLIGATION_COMMITTED = 1,
+    ASX_OBLIGATION_ABORTED   = 2,
+    ASX_OBLIGATION_LEAKED    = 3
+} asx_obligation_state;
+
+/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
+/* Outcome severity lattice                                           */
+/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
+
+typedef enum {
+    ASX_OUTCOME_OK        = 0,
+    ASX_OUTCOME_ERR       = 1,
+    ASX_OUTCOME_CANCELLED = 2,
+    ASX_OUTCOME_PANICKED  = 3
+} asx_outcome_severity;
+
+/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
+/* Cancellation kinds (11 variants, severity-ordered)                 */
+/* ------------------------------------------------------------------ */
+
+typedef enum {
+    ASX_CANCEL_USER         = 0,   /* severity 0 */
+    ASX_CANCEL_TIMEOUT      = 1,   /* severity 1 */
+    ASX_CANCEL_DEADLINE     = 2,   /* severity 1 */
+    ASX_CANCEL_POLL_QUOTA   = 3,   /* severity 2 */
+    ASX_CANCEL_COST_BUDGET  = 4,   /* severity 2 */
+    ASX_CANCEL_FAIL_FAST    = 5,   /* severity 3 */
+    ASX_CANCEL_RACE_LOST    = 6,   /* severity 3 */
+    ASX_CANCEL_LINKED_EXIT  = 7,   /* severity 3 */
+    ASX_CANCEL_PARENT       = 8,   /* severity 4 */
+    ASX_CANCEL_RESOURCE     = 9,   /* severity 4 */
+    ASX_CANCEL_SHUTDOWN     = 10   /* severity 5 */
+} asx_cancel_kind;
+
+/* Cancellation protocol phases */
+typedef enum {
+    ASX_CANCEL_PHASE_REQUESTED  = 0,
+    ASX_CANCEL_PHASE_CANCELLING = 1,
+    ASX_CANCEL_PHASE_FINALIZING = 2,
+    ASX_CANCEL_PHASE_COMPLETED  = 3
+} asx_cancel_phase;
+
+/* Return the severity level for a cancel kind (0-5) */
+static inline int asx_cancel_severity(asx_cancel_kind kind)
+{
+    static const int severity[] = {0, 1, 1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 4, 5};
+    return (int)kind <= 10 ? severity[(int)kind] : 5;
+}
+
 #endif /* ASX_IDS_H */

@@ -13,33 +13,10 @@
 
 #include <asx/asx_export.h>
 #include <asx/asx_status.h>
+#include <asx/asx_ids.h>
 
-/* Region lifecycle states */
-typedef enum {
-    ASX_REGION_OPEN       = 0,
-    ASX_REGION_CLOSING    = 1,
-    ASX_REGION_DRAINING   = 2,
-    ASX_REGION_FINALIZING = 3,
-    ASX_REGION_CLOSED     = 4
-} asx_region_state;
-
-/* Task lifecycle states */
-typedef enum {
-    ASX_TASK_CREATED          = 0,
-    ASX_TASK_RUNNING          = 1,
-    ASX_TASK_CANCEL_REQUESTED = 2,
-    ASX_TASK_CANCELLING       = 3,
-    ASX_TASK_FINALIZING       = 4,
-    ASX_TASK_COMPLETED        = 5
-} asx_task_state;
-
-/* Obligation lifecycle states */
-typedef enum {
-    ASX_OBLIGATION_RESERVED  = 0,
-    ASX_OBLIGATION_COMMITTED = 1,
-    ASX_OBLIGATION_ABORTED   = 2,
-    ASX_OBLIGATION_LEAKED    = 3
-} asx_obligation_state;
+/* State enums (asx_region_state, asx_task_state, asx_obligation_state)
+ * are defined in asx_ids.h. */
 
 /* Validate a region state transition. Returns ASX_OK or ASX_E_INVALID_TRANSITION. */
 ASX_API asx_status asx_region_transition_check(asx_region_state from, asx_region_state to);

-#include "test_harness.h"
+#include "../../test_harness.h"
 #include <asx/asx_status.h>
 
 TEST(ok_is_not_error) {
